skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5–120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent for short-form video production (5-120 seconds)
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Skill executes bash scripts to interact with Pexo AI video API. Static scanner detected external commands and env access patterns typical of API client scripts. Evaluation confirms these patterns are legitimate API client operations: scripts execute hardcoded commands with validated inputs, access config file for API credentials, and make network requests to Pexo API endpoints. Hidden file access (e.g., ~/.pexo/config) is standard practice for config management. No user input injection vectors found. High count of findings is due to documentation files (SETUP-CHECKLIST.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md) containing shell examples, not executable code.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Usage (MD5)
high_findings.description: MD5 hash functions used in shell scripts for temporary file naming. MD5 is cryptographically weak but used here for non-security purposes (temp file generation), not authentication or integrity verification.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 usage found but only for temp file naming, not security-critical operations. Low risk in this context.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Scripts
medium_findings.description: Scripts use bash command substitution $(...) for API interactions, file operations, and system diagnostics. Commands are hardcoded with no user input injection vectors.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution patterns are standard for API client scripts. All commands use validated inputs, no user-controlled command concatenation.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Configuration File Access
medium_findings.description: Scripts read configuration from ~/.pexo/config. Standard practice for API client credentials storage, not malicious file access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Config file access is standard for API client scripts. File path is hardcoded, not user-controlled.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Credentials
medium_findings.description: Scripts read PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL from environment or config file. Standard credential management pattern for API clients.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Credential access is legitimate API client pattern. No exfiltration detected - credentials only sent to Pexo API endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests to Hardcoded URLs
medium_findings.description: Scripts make HTTP requests to https://pexo.ai and related endpoints. Hardcoded URLs are expected for API clients.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests go to legitimate Pexo API. No suspicious external endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Patterns in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation files contain shell commands checking system state (curl, jq availability). These are troubleshooting instructions for users, not malicious reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands are in documentation (TROUBLESHOOTING.md), not executable code. Standard diagnostic patterns for setup guides.

low_findings.title: Standard Device File Access
low_findings.description: Scripts access /dev/null and standard streams. Normal shell scripting practice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard file descriptors (/dev/null, stdin/stdout/stderr) are normal shell operations.

content.user_title: Create AI Videos with Pexo Agent
content.value_statement: Creating professional short videos requires specialized tools and significant time. Pexo Agent automates scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music selection, producing polished 5-120 second videos in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Produce 5-120 second videos with 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 aspect ratios
Accept image, video, and audio uploads as creative references
Handle multi-turn conversations for video revisions and refinements
Generate preview options and deliver final video with download URL
Support multiple languages in user communication
limitations: Cannot crawl web URLs or download files from internet
Video production takes 15-20 minutes for short clips
Asset tags are mandatory for file references

use_cases.title: Marketing Teams Create Product Videos
use_cases.description: Marketing teams upload product images and request promotional videos for social media campaigns. Pexo handles shot composition and transitions.

use_cases.title: Content Creators Generate Social Clips
use_cases.description: YouTubers and TikTok creators provide reference images and ask for video variations. Pexo generates multiple preview options to choose from.

use_cases.title: Designers Get Quick Video Explainer
use_cases.description: Designers upload wireframes or mockups and request explainer videos. Pexo interprets visual references and produces corresponding video content.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Video Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 20-second product video showing our new wireless headphones with energetic music
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants a basic promotional video with specific duration

prompt_templates.title: Video with Image Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TikTok-style video using the attached product photo as the main subject with bright, modern styling
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides an image file to use as video subject

prompt_templates.title: Revision Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make the transition slower and add more dramatic music
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to modify an existing video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-File Video Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the logo image for branding and the product video as the main content, create a brand intro clip
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides multiple files to combine in video

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second product ad for sneakers with an urban street style
output_examples.output:
Your video is ready! Download: https://pexo-assets.oss-us-east-1.aliyuncs.com/projects/abc123/assets/video.mp4

Project page: https://pexo.ai/project/abc123

output_examples.input: Make a tutorial explainer using the uploaded slides
output_examples.output:
Here are 3 preview options:
A: https://pexo-assets...video_a.mp4
B: https://pexo-assets...video_b.mp4
C: https://pexo-assets...video_c.mp4

Which style do you prefer?

best_practices: Upload image or video files as references when providing visual content
Pass user requests exactly as written to preserve their intent
Wait at least 60 seconds between status polls to avoid rate limiting
anti_patterns: Do not add creative direction the user did not specify - Pexo handles artistic choices
Do not send chat messages during WAIT status - this triggers duplicate production
Do not wrap download URLs in markdown links - some platforms break the URL

faq.question: What video formats and lengths does Pexo support?
faq.answer: Pexo produces videos 5-120 seconds long with 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (vertical), or 1:1 (square) aspect ratios.

faq.question: What file types can I upload as references?
faq.answer: You can upload images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), videos (mp4, mov, avi), and audio files (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac).

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: Simple 15-second videos typically take 15-20 minutes. Complex videos or longer clips take longer.

faq.question: Can I request revisions to a video?
faq.answer: Yes, send your feedback using the same project_id. Pexo will incorporate your suggestions into a new version.

faq.question: What is a project_id and where do I find it?
faq.answer: The project_id is created automatically when you request a new video. It is returned by pexo-project-create.sh and shown in the project URL.

faq.question: How do I handle credit or billing errors?
faq.answer: If a command fails with a credits error, Pexo automatically displays purchase instructions. Visit pexo.ai to add credits.
